mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 65% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative mean forward return or a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 55% of observations when industrial production rises while CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 2.5% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 over a 5-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative information coefficient or positive return frequency below 45% across rolling 36-month evaluation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward equity returns or produces negative returns in over 60% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month consecutive decline in the industrial production index signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of cases following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
